Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to construct an accepting Petri net for the described insurance claim process. The process has activities: CR, DV, FC, CA, AP, P, N, CL. There are loops: after DV, if documents incomplete, go to RDS (re-document submission) and then back to DV? Or after RDS, go back to DV? The description says: if documents are incomplete after Document Verification (DV), a re-submission of documents step occurs (Re-Document Submission (RDS)) before the process continues. So after DV, a decision: either continue (to FC) or go to RDS. After RDS, presumably go back to DV to verify again? Or maybe RDS leads to DV again? Typically, after re-submission, you need re-verification. So loop: DV -> RDS -> DV. Similarly, after FC, if doubts, go to II (In-depth Investigation) which can happen multiple times before proceeding to CA. So loop: FC -> II -> FC (or II leads back to FC).

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also note: after FC, go to CA. After CA, there is a threshold: if amount surpasses threshold, need Approval (AP), then Payment (P). Otherwise, perhaps directly to Payment? The description: Approval (AP): The claim must be approved by a senior manager if the amount surpasses a certain threshold. So if not, skip AP.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: But in Petri nets, we can model a choice place after CA: two transitions, one labeled AP (if threshold) and one labeled maybe Skip Approval? that goes directly to the place before P. But the description says the activities are only those eight. Possibly we can model the decision as a silent transition (tau) or just have two outgoing arcs from the same place: one to AP then to P, and one to P directly. But the transition from CA to P without AP would be a transition with no label? In Petri net, transitions can be silent (invisible). In pm4py, we can create a transition with label None or empty string. Usually, to represent decisions, we use invisible transitions.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: To model the loop, we can have a place that is both the output of the loop activity and input to the original activity, but then the original activity has that place as input. However, if the original activity already has a single input from the main path, we can make it have two input places: one from main path and one from loop, but then it needs both tokens to fire, which is wrong. Better: use a place as the input to FC that collects tokens from both main path and loop. That means we need a transition from main path to that place, and a transition from loop to that place. The main path transition can be a silent transition. Actually, after DV, we have a place p_after_DV. Instead of sending directly to FC, we can have a silent transition (tau1) from p_after_DV to p_before_FC. Then FC takes token from p_before_FC and outputs to p_after_FC. Then from p_after_FC, we have CA (go to next) or II (go to p_before_FC).

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Places: p_start: initial token. p_after_CR: after CR, before DV. p_after_DV: after DV, before decision. p_before_FC: input to FC (collects from main path and II loop). p_after_FC: after FC, before decision (CA or II). p_after_CA: after CA, before approval decision. p_before_P: input to P (collects from AP or skip). p_after_P: after P, before N. p_after_N: after N, before CL. p_after_CL: final place.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Arcs: CR: input start, output after_CR. DV: input after_CR, output after_DV. From after_DV, two outgoing transitions: tau1 and RDS. tau1 output to before_FC. RDS output to after_CR. FC: input before_FC, output after_FC. From after_FC, two outgoing transitions: CA and II. CA output to after_CA. II output to before_FC. From after_CA, two outgoing transitions: AP and tau2. AP output to before_P. tau2 output to before_P. P: input before_P, output after_P. N: input after_P, output after_N. CL: input after_N, output after_CL.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Now we need to implement in Python using pm4py. We will create the PetriNet object, add places and transitions, add arcs using petri_utils.add_arc_from_to. Then define im and fm.
